---
title: "LVEF"
author: "Omar Abdullah Gill"
date: "2026-04-07"
output: html_document
---
```{r}
pacman::p_load(bayesmeta,          # Bayesian Meta-Analysis
               BayesianPriorsTool, # Priors Building Tool
               dplyr,              # Data Wrangle
               tidyr,              # Data Wrangle
               meta,               # Forest Plot
               metafor,            # Effect Sizes
               ggdist,             # Distributions
               ggplot2,            # Plots
               patchwork,          # Plots
               here,
               readxl,             # Import data
               gt)  



# ggplot2 theme
theme_set(
  theme(
    plot.title.position = 'plot',
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 14),
    panel.background = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "gray80", linewidth = 0.3)
  )
)
```


```{r}
# Create the dataset
lvef_df <- data.frame(
  subgroup = c(rep("HBP", 4), rep("LBAP", 4)),
  study = c("Huang, 2022", "Lustgarten, 2015", "Upadhyay, 2019", "Vinther, 2021",
            "Pujol-Lopez, 2022", "Vijayaraman, 2023", "Wang, 2022", "Physio-Synch Trial, 2026"),
  # CSP Group Data
  m1 = c(53.90, 32.00, 36.90, 46.00, 12.20, 42.90, 49.40,35),
  sd1   = c(11.90, 7.50, 11.50, 9.00, 9.00, 10.10, 13.20, 12),
  n1    = c(18, 12, 16, 25, 35, 50, 20, 87),
  # BVP Group Data
  m2 = c(51.30, 31.00, 34.50, 43.00, 13.10, 39.10, 46.50, 39),
  sd2   = c(7.40, 5.50, 7.20, 7.00, 9.00, 9.70, 9.40, 12),
  n2    = c(20, 12, 24, 25, 33, 50, 20, 86)
)


dat_lvef_MD <- escalc(measure = "MD", 
                     m1i = m1, sd1i = sd1, n1i = n1, 
                     m2i = m2, sd2i = sd2, n2i = n2, 
                     data = lvef_df) |> 
  data.frame()

# Calculate Standard Error (sei) from Variance (vi)
dat_lvef_MD$sei <- sqrt(dat_lvef_MD$vi)

# View the analyzed dataset
print(dat_lvef_MD[, c("study", "subgroup", "yi", "vi", "sei")])
```


```{r}
# 1. Define Priors for Mean Difference (mu)
# Using your specified vague prior (Mean 0, SD 51)
prior_mu_vague_mean_lvef = 0
prior_mu_vague_sd_lvef   = 51

# 2. Define Prior for Heterogeneity (tau)
# Setting the scale to 12 as per your tau_prior_plot request
weakly_prior_MD_scale_lvef = 12

# Defining the half-normal function for the bayesmeta call
prior_tau_weakly_lvef = function(x) dhalfnormal(x, scale = weakly_prior_MD_scale_lvef)
# 3. Visualization of Priors
# Visualizing the distribution of the Mean Difference prior
mu_dist_viz(mean   = prior_mu_vague_mean_lvef, 
            sd     = prior_mu_vague_sd_lvef,
            scale  = "absolute",
            inner_color = "blue", 
            xlabel = "Mean Difference ( LVEF %)")


# Visualizing the Weakly Informative tau prior
tau_prior_plot(weakly_prior_MD_scale_lvef)


# 4. Run the Bayesian Meta-Analysis
# This links your calculated effect sizes (yi) and standard errors (sei)
model_lvef_primary = bayesmeta(
  y      = dat_lvef_MD$yi,           # Mean Difference from escalc
  sigma  = dat_lvef_MD$sei,          # Standard Error from escalc
  labels = dat_lvef_MD$study,        # Study Names
  
  # Applying the synchronized priors
  mu.prior.mean = prior_mu_vague_mean_lvef,
  mu.prior.sd   = prior_mu_vague_sd_lvef,
  tau.prior     = prior_tau_weakly_lvef
)

# 5. Review Results
summary(model_lvef_primary)
```

#sub-group analysis
```{r}
# Ensure subgroup is a factor with HBP as the reference
dat_lvef_MD$subgroup = factor(dat_lvef_MD$subgroup, 
                             levels = c("HBP", "LBAP"))

# Create the model matrix for the regression
# This creates an Intercept (HBP) and a coefficient for LBAP
matrix_qrs_subgroups = stats::model.matrix(~subgroup, data = dat_lvef_MD)



# Priors for the coefficients (Beta)
# Index 1: Intercept (HBP Mean Difference)
# Index 2: Slope (Difference between LBAP and HBP)
prior_beta_weakly_mean_lvef = 0
prior_beta_weakly_sd_lvef = 12 # Weakly informative for the difference in ms


lvef_priors_means = c(prior_mu_vague_mean_lvef, prior_beta_weakly_mean_lvef)
lvef_priors_sd    = c(prior_mu_vague_sd_lvef,   prior_beta_weakly_sd_lvef)


#main analysis

subgroup_lvef_model = bmr(
  y      = dat_lvef_MD$yi,            # Mean Difference (ms)
  sigma  = dat_lvef_MD$sei,           # Standard Error
  labels = dat_lvef_MD$study,         # Study names (e.g., Huang, 2022)
  X      = matrix_qrs_subgroups,     # Subgroup matrix (HBP vs LBAP)
  
  # Applying the synchronized priors
  beta.prior.mean = lvef_priors_means, 
  beta.prior.sd   = lvef_priors_sd,
  
  # Using the Informative tau prior (scale = 4) for the primary subgroup model
  tau.prior = prior_tau_weakly_lvef 
)

# View results
summary(subgroup_lvef_model)
```


```{r}
# 1. Extract Posterior Samples from SUBGROUP Model (for Subtotals) --------
set.seed(123)

subgroup_samples = 
  subgroup_lvef_model$rposterior(n = 10000, tau.sample = FALSE) |> 
  data.frame() |> 
  reframe(HBP = `X.Intercept.`,
          LBAP = `X.Intercept.` + subgroupLBAP) # Adjust to your slope name

# Get the mean and 95% CrI for each subgroup
subgroupHBP_summary = subgroup_samples$HBP |> mean_hdi()
subgroupLBAP_summary = subgroup_samples$LBAP |> mean_hdi()

posterior_subgroup_lvef_model = 
  data.frame(
    subgroupHBP_mean = subgroupHBP_summary$y,
    subgroupHBP_upper = subgroupHBP_summary$ymax,
    subgroupHBP_lower = subgroupHBP_summary$ymin,
    
    subgroupLBAP_mean = subgroupLBAP_summary$y,
    subgroupLBAP_upper = subgroupLBAP_summary$ymax,
    subgroupLBAP_lower = subgroupLBAP_summary$ymin,
    
    tau_median = subgroup_lvef_model$summary["median","tau"],  
    tau_upper = subgroup_lvef_model$summary["95% upper","tau"],
    tau_lower = subgroup_lvef_model$summary["95% lower","tau"]
  ) 

# 2. Extract Posterior Samples from OVERALL Model (model_qrs_primary) -----
# This ensures the 'Total' diamond matches your primary analysis
overall_samples = 
  model_lvef_primary$rposterior(n = 10000, tau.sample = FALSE) 

prediction_samples = 
  model_lvef_primary$rposterior(n = 10000, tau.sample = FALSE, predict = TRUE)

overall_summary_lvef = overall_samples |> mean_hdi()
prediction_summary_lvef = prediction_samples |> mean_hdi()


# 3. Create Forest Plot Object --------------------------------------------
forest_data_MD = metacont(
  data = dat_lvef_MD,
  subgroup = subgroup,   
  studlab = study,
  mean.e = m1, sd.e = sd1, n.e = n1, # CSP
  mean.c = m2, sd.c = sd2, n.c = n2, # BVP
  sm = "MD", common = FALSE, random = FALSE
)

# 4. Add the Overall Bayesian Results -------------------------------------
m1_lvef = metaadd(forest_data_MD,
                 type = "random", text = "Overall Results",
                 TE = overall_summary_lvef$y,
                 lower = overall_summary_lvef$ymin,
                 upper = overall_summary_lvef$ymax)

# Manually insert subgroup-specific results (Subtotals)
m1_lvef$TE.random.w = c(posterior_subgroup_lvef_model$subgroupHBP_mean,
                       posterior_subgroup_lvef_model$subgroupLBAP_mean)

m1_lvef$lower.random.w = c(posterior_subgroup_lvef_model$subgroupHBP_lower,
                          posterior_subgroup_lvef_model$subgroupLBAP_lower)

m1_lvef$upper.random.w = c(posterior_subgroup_lvef_model$subgroupHBP_upper,
                          posterior_subgroup_lvef_model$subgroupLBAP_upper)

# Name the subtotals
names(m1_lvef$TE.random.w) <- names(m1_lvef$lower.random.w) <- 
  names(m1_lvef$upper.random.w) <-  c("HBP", "LBAP")

# Add the Overall Prediction Interval
m1_lvef_predict = metaadd(
  m1_lvef,
  type = "prediction",
  TE = prediction_summary_lvef$y,
  upper = prediction_summary_lvef$ymax,
  lower = prediction_summary_lvef$ymin
)  

# 5. Calculation of Subgroup Probabilities --------------------------------
prob_lower_than_0 = subgroup_lvef_model$pposterior(which.beta = 2, beta = 0)
prob_greater_than_0 = 1 - prob_lower_than_0
chosen_prob_lvef = max(prob_lower_than_0, prob_greater_than_0)

subgroup_diff_text = 
  paste0("Posterior Probability of Subgroup Difference = ", 
         round(chosen_prob_lvef, 3)*100, "%")

tau_subgroups_text = 
  paste0("Heterogeneity (\u03C4) not explained by subgroups = ", 
         round(posterior_subgroup_lvef_model$tau_median, 2),
         " [95% CrI ",
         round(posterior_subgroup_lvef_model$tau_lower, 2),
         ", ",
         round(posterior_subgroup_lvef_model$tau_upper, 2), "]")

# 6. Final Plotting -------------------------------------------------------
forest(m1_lvef_predict,
       layout = "RevMan5",
       sortvar = TE,
       text.random = "Total (95% CrI)",
       text.predict = "95% Prediction Interval",
       text.random.w = "Subtotal (95% CrI)",
       col.subgroup = "black",
       print.subgroup.name = FALSE,
       hetstat = FALSE,
       test.subgroup = FALSE,
       
       text.addline1 = subgroup_diff_text,
       text.addline2 = tau_subgroups_text,
       
       label.e = "CSP", lab.c = "BVP",
       label.left = "Favors BVP", label.right = "Favors CSP",
       
       smlab = "Mean Difference (ms)",
       leftcols = c("studlab", "n.e", "mean.e", "sd.e", "n.c", "mean.c", "sd.c", "effect.ci"),
       digits.mean = 1, digits.sd = 1, colgap = "3mm")
```


```{r}
set.seed(123)

# 1. Extract Posterior Samples --------------------------------------------
# Using your subgroup model and overall model objects
subgroup_samples_lvef = 
  subgroup_lvef_model$rposterior(n = 10000, tau.sample = FALSE) |> 
  data.frame() |> 
  reframe(
    HBP = `X.Intercept.`,                # Intercept = HBP
    LBAP = `X.Intercept.` + subgroupLBAP # Intercept + Slope = LBAP
  )


# Extract samples for overall model
overall_samples_lvef = model_lvef_primary$rposterior(n = 10000, tau.sample = FALSE)

# Combine into one data frame
combined_samples_lvef = subgroup_samples_lvef |> 
  mutate(Overall = overall_samples_lvef)

# 2. Density Plot (Half-Eye) ----------------------------------------------
adensity_plot_lvef = 
  combined_samples_lvef |> 
  pivot_longer(everything()) |> 
  mutate(name = factor(name, levels = c("HBP", "LBAP", "Overall"))) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = value, fill = name)) +
  # Shows distribution + Mean + 95% HDI
  stat_halfeye(.width = 0.95, point_interval = mean_hdi) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#BE6376", "#EECE9C", "#605A91")) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") + # Reference is 0ms
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-7, 20, 7)) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-10, 10)) +
  labs(x = "\nMean Difference in LVEF (%)", y = "") +
  facet_wrap(~name, ncol = 1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "none",
        strip.text.x = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank())

# 3. ECDF (Cumulative Probability) Plot -----------------------------------
aprobability_plot_lvef = 
  combined_samples_lvef |> 
  pivot_longer(everything()) |> 
  mutate(name = factor(name, levels = c("HBP", "LBAP", "Overall"))) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = value, colour = name)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = after_stat(y)), stat = 'ecdf', linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#BE6376", "#EECE9C", "#605A91")) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-7, 20, 7)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1), name = "Cumulative Probability") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-10, 10)) +
  labs(x = "\nMean Difference in LVEF (%)") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "gray90", linewidth = 0.3),
        legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
        legend.title = element_blank())

# 4. Combine Plots --------------------------------------------------------
adensity_plot_lvef + aprobability_plot_lvef
```


```{r}

# --- LVEF POSTERIOR SUMMARY TABLE ---

# 1. Calculation & Extraction
# We use mean_hdi to get the Bayesian point estimate and 95% Credible Interval
lvef_hdi_summary <- mean_hdi(overall_samples, .width = 0.95)

# 2. Probability of Benefit
# P(Mean Difference > 0)
prob_benefit_lvef <- (1 - model_lvef_primary$pposterior(mu = 0)) * 100

# 3. Create the Tibble
lvef_viewer_df <- tibble(
  Outcome = "Left Ventricular Ejection Fraction (LVEF)",
  `Effect Size` = "Mean Difference (%)",
  mu_mean = lvef_hdi_summary$y,
  mu_lower = lvef_hdi_summary$ymin,
  mu_upper = lvef_hdi_summary$ymax,
  `Probability of Benefit (%)` = round(prob_benefit_lvef, 1)
) |> 
  mutate(`Mean [95% Credible Interval]` = paste0(
    round(mu_mean, 1), " [", 
    round(mu_lower, 1), ", ", 
    round(mu_upper, 1), "]"
  ))

# 4. Generate the GT Table for the Viewer
lvef_viewer_df |> 
  select(Outcome, `Effect Size`, `Mean [95% Credible Interval]`, `Probability of Benefit (%)`) |> 
  gt() |> 
  # --- Styling ---
  tab_header(
    title = md("**Bayesian Posterior Summary: LVEF Analysis**"),
    subtitle = "Primary Analysis using Vague Mu and Weakly Informative Tau Priors"
  ) |> 
  cols_align(align = "center", columns = everything()) |> 
  cols_label(
    `Probability of Benefit (%)` = md("**Probability of Benefit (%)**")
  ) |> 
  tab_options(
    table.width = px(600),
    column_labels.font.weight = "bold",
    heading.title.font.size = px(18)
  ) |> 
  tab_footnote(
    footnote = "Posterior Probability that Mean Difference > 0 (Favors CSP over BVP).",
    locations = cells_column_labels(columns = `Probability of Benefit (%)`)
  ) |> 
  tab_footnote(
    footnote = "95% Credible Interval represents the Highest Density Interval (HDI).",
    locations = cells_column_labels(columns = `Mean [95% Credible Interval]`)
  )

```


```{r}
# 1. Parameter Definitions
prior_mu_vague_mean_lvef = 0
prior_mu_vague_sd_lvef   = 51
weakly_prior_MD_scale_lvef = 12

# 2. Build the Prior Summary Tibble
lvef_prior_summary <- tibble(
  Outcome = "LVEF",
  `Effect Size` = "Mean Difference (%)",
  `Prior Type for Mu` = "Vague",
  mu_mean = prior_mu_vague_mean_lvef,
  mu_sd = prior_mu_vague_sd_lvef,
  `Prior Type for Tau` = "Weakly-informative",
  tau_mean = 0,
  tau_sd = weakly_prior_MD_scale_lvef
) |> 
  mutate(
    `Mu Prior Distribution` = paste0("Normal(", mu_mean, ", ", mu_sd, ")"),
    `Tau Prior Distribution` = paste0("Half-Normal(", tau_sd, ")")
  )

# 3. Create the GT Table
lvef_prior_summary |> 
  select(Outcome, `Effect Size`, `Prior Type for Mu`, `Mu Prior Distribution`, 
         `Prior Type for Tau`, `Tau Prior Distribution`) |> 
  gt() |> 
  tab_header(title = "Bayesian Prior Specifications for LVEF Analysis") |> 
  tab_footnote(
    footnote = "Mu represents the overall pooled effect (Mean Difference).",
    locations = cells_column_labels(columns = `Mu Prior Distribution`)
  ) |> 
  tab_footnote(
    footnote = "Tau represents between-study heterogeneity. Half-Normal is truncated at 0.",
    locations = cells_column_labels(columns = `Tau Prior Distribution`)
  )




```
